local_names.romanized_local: Pin Ke Yuan Wei Shu Pian (Mandarin romanization)

aliases: Pin Ke Yuan Wei Shu Pian

romanized: Pin Ke Yuan Wei Shu Pian

region_of_origin: USA (developed by P&G 1968; now Kellanova/Mars); sold in Singapore by Kellanova Singapore
food_subgroup: Potato crisps — reconstituted dehydrated potato, commercially fried, NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Pringles or reconstituted potato crisps specifically. Closest parent would be potato chip / crisp category; URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0HAG is the closest defensible parent for commercial potato crisps. No specific code exists for reconstituted/formed potato crisps (Pringles-type).
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Pringles or reconstituted potato crisps in clinical terminology databases.

serving.natural_language_portion: One label serving of Pringles Original is 28g (approximately 15 chips), providing ~150 kcal, 9g fat, 16g carbohydrate, and ~170mg sodium; however, realistic consumption at a sitting is typically half a can (55g) or more, making portion control the primary clinical concern. A half-can (55g) provides ~295 kcal, 17g fat, and 335mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Pringles Original — Kellanova Singapore Nutrition Label and USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from Kellanova Singapore product label (primary) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Pringles Original. Values are per 100g, derived from the 28g label serving data scaled to 100g. Both sources are consistent. USDA Branded Foods is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy for packaged/commercial products.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — potato crisps (reconstituted/formed type); Atkinson et al. 2008 and University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~77 for Pringles-type reconstituted potato crisps is referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Reconstituted dehydrated potato flake chips have a higher GI than whole-potato sliced crisps (e.g., Lay's ~54) because the dehydration-reconstitution process disrupts the native starch granule structure, increasing gelatinisation and amylase accessibility. GL per 28g label serving = (77 × 16g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 12.3, rounded to 11.6 per input source. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — 607mg per 100g; ~170mg per 28g label serving; ~335mg per realistic half-can (55g)
hypertension.guidance: Pringles Original delivers ~170mg sodium per label serving (28g), which is moderate in isolation, but portion escalation to half a can (55g) raises sodium intake to ~335mg per snack occasion; hypertensive patients should strictly limit to the label serving and account for cumulative daily sodium from all sources, targeting under 2000mg/day per Singapore HPB guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI ~77) from reconstituted potato starch; rapid postprandial glucose spike; low satiety per calorie
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pringles Original has a high GI of ~77 due to the reconstituted dehydrated potato starch structure, which is rapidly digested; the glycemic load per label serving is ~12 (moderate) but rises to ~23 at realistic half-can consumption (high), making this a poor snack choice for T2DM management — patients should avoid or strictly limit to 15 chips counted out, and prefer lower-GI alternatives such as a small handful of mixed nuts (~28g, GI <20) for better glycaemic and satiety outcomes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (8.9g/100g), high energy density (536 kcal/100g), NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed, high sodium, and potential acrylamide from reconstituted starch frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pringles Original is a NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed food with 8.9g saturated fat per 100g (44% of daily limit in a 100g portion), 607mg sodium per 100g, and 536 kcal per 100g; regular consumption is associated with increased cardiovascular risk through saturated fat-driven LDL elevation, sodium-driven blood pressure effects, and the pro-inflammatory profile of ultra-processed foods — CVD patients should treat Pringles as an occasional indulgence only, strictly limiting to the 28g label serving and preferring whole-food snacks such as unsalted nuts or raw vegetables.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High GI, NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed, potential acrylamide from reconstituted starch frying (Maillard reaction product)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For rheumatoid arthritis patients, Pringles Original is among the less appropriate snack choices due to its high GI (pro-inflammatory glucose-insulin cycling), ultra-processed formulation, and potential acrylamide content from frying reconstituted potato starch at high temperatures; occasional consumption is clinically acceptable but regular snacking should be replaced with anti-inflammatory alternatives such as nuts, seeds, or whole-grain crackers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pringles Original Potato Crisps are a globally recognised NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed snack food manufactured by Kellanova (formerly Kellogg's, originally P&G) and sold in Singapore in a standard 110g stackable can. Unlike conventional potato crisps made from sliced whole potatoes (e.g., Lay's), Pringles are made from a dough of dehydrated potato flakes, wheat starch, rice flour, and vegetable oil that is formed into a uniform saddle shape and fried. This reconstitution process disrupts the native starch granule structure, resulting in a significantly higher glycemic index (~77, HIGH) compared to whole-potato sliced crisps (~54). Per 100g: 536 kcal, 5.4g protein, 32.1g fat (8.9g saturated), 57.1g carbohydrate (1.4g sugar, 3.6g fibre), 607mg sodium. The label serving is 28g (~15 chips, ~150 kcal, ~170mg sodium), but realistic consumption at a single sitting is typically half a can (55g, ~295 kcal, ~335mg sodium, GL ~23) or a full can (110g, ~590 kcal). The texture is uniformly crisp and light with a mild salted potato flavour. Clinically, Pringles present three primary concerns: (1) high GI driving rapid postprandial glucose spikes, particularly problematic for T2DM; (2) high saturated fat and sodium density relevant to CVD and hypertension; and (3) the ultra-processed formulation associated with pro-inflammatory dietary patterns relevant to RA and metabolic syndrome. Portion control is the dominant clinical intervention. Source: Kellanova Singapore label + USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from Kellanova Singapore product label (primary) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (approved secondary source for packaged products). Both sources are consistent. GI value of ~77 is referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008) for reconstituted potato crisp products. All values are well-supported with confidence 1.0 for macronutrients and 0.9 for GI.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Kellanova Singapore label and is not available in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this specific product as of April 2026. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content for this product is not reported on the Singapore label or in the USDA Branded Foods entry consulted. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per Kellanova Singapore label declaration). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — product contains no animal-derived fat; vegetable oil only). vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0 (confirmed zero or not present per label and USDA source; these vitamins are not added to Pringles Original and are not naturally present in the fried potato-starch matrix at detectable levels). 3. GI ~77 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) for reconstituted/formed potato crisps; confidence 0.9. GL per 28g label serving calculated as (77 × 16g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 12.3 (input source reports 11.6; minor rounding difference retained from input). 4. FoodEx2 code A0HAG is the closest defensible parent for commercial potato crisps; no specific FoodEx2 code exists for reconstituted/formed potato crisps. FoodOn URI not assigned — no exact or close FoodOn URI identified without risk of fabrication. 5. This is a packaged commercial product (NOVA Class 4); the Singapore HPB Foodo database does not carry branded packaged snack products, so USDA Branded Foods is the appropriate approved source per Project Veritas v2 source policy. 6. Multilingual names provided for Singapore distribution context (English, Chinese Simplified/Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this product is widely sold in Singapore retail.
